key_insight: PEFT methods should be judged not only by downstream accuracy, but by a stability-plasticity trade-off between task adaptation and preservation of pretrained capabilities.
engineering_relevance: For large-model adaptation workflows, this provides a practical way to choose finetuning schemes that improve the target task without eroding general-purpose performance.
method: Introduced PEFT-Arena, a benchmark that jointly measures downstream task performance and general-capability retention.
Compared PEFT methods via stability-plasticity profiles and Pareto fronts under similar parameter budgets.
Analyzed updates in weight space and activation space, then tested post-hoc improvement with path-wise rewinding.
evidence_signal: Across the benchmark, methods showed distinct stability-plasticity profiles, and orthogonal finetuning achieved the most favorable Pareto frontier under comparable parameter budgets.
watch_item: Retention gains depend on the chosen benchmark and representation metrics, so reported improvements may not transfer uniformly across tasks or model families.
paper_title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
